text: What is the main reason cells stay relatively small?
choices: Smaller cells maintain a higher surface area-to-volume ratio, improving exchange with the environment
Small cells do not need energy
explain: Cells stay relatively small because a higher surface area-to-volume ratio allows faster exchange of materials with the environment.
choice_explanations: Incorrect. DNA amount is not the main reason cells stay small.
Correct. A high surface area-to-volume ratio helps cells move materials in and out efficiently.
Incorrect. Small cells still require energy.
Incorrect. Many large cells do contain organelles.

text: What is the plasma membrane mainly made of?
choices: A phospholipid bilayer with embedded proteins
A cellulose wall with attached DNA
explain: The plasma membrane is mainly a phospholipid bilayer with proteins embedded in or associated with it.
choice_explanations: Correct. The cell membrane is primarily a phospholipid bilayer with proteins and other components.
Incorrect. That describes plant cell wall material, not the plasma membrane.
Incorrect. Membranes are not made from amino acid bilayers.
Incorrect. Nucleotides make nucleic acids, not membranes.

text: What does selective permeability mean?
choices: The membrane lets all substances pass equally
The membrane regulates which substances can cross more easily than others
Only water can cross the membrane
The membrane works only in plant cells
explain: Selective permeability means the membrane controls which substances cross and how easily they do so.
choice_explanations: Incorrect. Membranes do not allow all substances through equally.
Correct. Selective permeability is a core property of biological membranes.
Incorrect. Many substances can cross, depending on their properties and transport mechanisms.
Incorrect. Selective permeability applies to all cells.

text: What is diffusion?
choices: Movement of molecules from high concentration to low concentration
Movement of molecules from low concentration to high concentration using energy
Movement of water across a membrane only
explain: Diffusion is the passive movement of particles down their concentration gradient.
choice_explanations: Correct. Diffusion is passive movement down a concentration gradient.
Incorrect. That describes active transport.
Incorrect. That describes osmosis.
Incorrect. That is not diffusion.

text: What is osmosis?
choices: Diffusion of solutes through a membrane
Diffusion of water across a selectively permeable membrane
Movement of vesicles out of the cell
explain: Osmosis is the passive movement of water across a selectively permeable membrane.
choice_explanations: Incorrect. Osmosis specifically refers to water.
Correct. Osmosis is the passive movement of water.
Incorrect. Osmosis does not require ATP.
Incorrect. That is exocytosis.

text: Which organelle is the main site of ATP production in eukaryotic cells?
explain: Mitochondria are the major site of ATP production in eukaryotic cells.
choice_explanations: Incorrect. Ribosomes make proteins.
Incorrect. Golgi modifies and sorts molecules.
Correct. Mitochondria are the major site of ATP production.
Incorrect. Lysosomes mainly digest materials.

text: Which organelle contains digestive enzymes?
explain: Lysosomes contain enzymes used to digest macromolecules and recycle worn-out components.
choice_explanations: Correct. Lysosomes contain enzymes that break down macromolecules and worn-out cell parts.
Incorrect. Chloroplasts carry out photosynthesis.
Incorrect. The nucleus stores genetic information.
Incorrect. Smooth ER is involved in lipid synthesis and detoxification.

text: What is the main function of ribosomes?
explain: Ribosomes translate mRNA into polypeptides, making proteins.
choice_explanations: Incorrect. Ribosomes do not store lipids.
Correct. Ribosomes translate mRNA to make proteins.
Incorrect. Ribosomes do not replicate DNA.
Incorrect. Ribosomes primarily build proteins.

text: What is the function of the Golgi apparatus?
choices: To modify, sort, and package proteins and lipids
explain: The Golgi apparatus modifies, sorts, and packages many cellular products for delivery.
choice_explanations: Correct. The Golgi apparatus processes and ships cellular products.
Incorrect. DNA is replicated in the nucleus.
Incorrect. That is mainly the job of mitochondria.
Incorrect. That is not the main role of the Golgi.

text: What type of transport requires ATP?
explain: Active transport requires ATP because it moves substances against their gradients.
choice_explanations: Incorrect. Simple diffusion is passive.
Incorrect. Osmosis is passive.
Correct. Active transport moves substances against gradients using energy.
Incorrect. Facilitated diffusion uses transport proteins but not ATP directly.

text: Which structure is found in plant cells but not animal cells?
explain: A large central vacuole is a typical plant cell feature not found in animal cells.
choice_explanations: Correct. Plant cells typically have a large central vacuole.
Incorrect. Both plant and animal cells have plasma membranes.
Incorrect. Both plant and animal cells have ribosomes.
Incorrect. Both plant and animal cells have mitochondria.

text: What is exocytosis?
choices: Uptake of materials into the cell through vesicles
Release of materials from the cell through vesicle fusion with the membrane
Diffusion of water through a membrane
Protein production in the rough ER
explain: Exocytosis releases materials out of the cell when vesicles fuse with the plasma membrane.
choice_explanations: Incorrect. That is endocytosis.
Correct. Exocytosis exports substances out of the cell.
Incorrect. That is osmosis.
Incorrect. That is not exocytosis.

text: Why does an increase in cell size create a challenge for homeostasis?
choices: Larger cells always lose their nuclei
Volume increases faster than surface area, making exchange less efficient
Surface area disappears as cells grow
explain: As a cell grows, its volume increases faster than its surface area, lowering the surface area-to-volume ratio and reducing exchange efficiency.
choice_explanations: Incorrect. Larger cells do not necessarily lose nuclei.
Correct. As cells grow, surface area-to-volume ratio decreases, limiting transport efficiency.
Incorrect. Large cells still use ATP.
Incorrect. Surface area increases, but more slowly than volume.

text: A cell is placed in a hypertonic solution. What is the most likely outcome?
choices: Water enters the cell, causing swelling
Water leaves the cell, causing it to shrink
Solutes leave the cell by active transport only
explain: In a hypertonic solution, water leaves the cell by osmosis, causing the cell to shrink.
choice_explanations: Incorrect. In a hypertonic solution, water tends to leave the cell.
Correct. Hypertonic environments have lower water potential relative to the cell.
Incorrect. The key immediate effect is water movement.
Incorrect. Simply placing a cell in hypertonic solution does not make it isotonic.

text: A transport protein allows glucose to move into a cell down its concentration gradient. Which process is occurring?
explain: Glucose is moving down its gradient through a membrane protein, which is facilitated diffusion.
choice_explanations: Incorrect. Active transport requires ATP to move against a gradient.
Correct. A transport protein helps glucose move down its gradient without ATP.
Incorrect. Exocytosis exports materials in vesicles.
Incorrect. Endocytosis brings in large materials via vesicles.

text: Why are phospholipids well suited for membrane formation?
choices: They have hydrophilic heads and hydrophobic tails, allowing bilayer formation in water
They are made of amino acids
explain: Phospholipids are amphipathic, so they self-assemble into bilayers in aqueous environments.
choice_explanations: Incorrect. Phospholipids are amphipathic, not entirely hydrophobic.
Correct. This structure drives self-assembly into membranes.
Incorrect. Phospholipids are lipids, not proteins.
Incorrect. Nucleic acids store hereditary information.

text: A molecule crosses the membrane rapidly when the concentration outside the cell is higher than inside, but the movement stops when concentrations become equal. Which process is most likely?
explain: Passive diffusion continues until equilibrium is reached, then there is no net movement.
choice_explanations: Correct. Passive diffusion continues until equilibrium is reached.
Incorrect. Active transport would require ATP and could continue against equilibrium.
Incorrect. Endocytosis uses vesicles for large materials.
Incorrect. Exocytosis releases materials from the cell.

text: Which feature of the rough ER best explains its role in protein processing?
choices: It is covered with ribosomes that synthesize proteins destined for membranes or export
explain: The rough ER is studded with ribosomes that synthesize proteins entering the endomembrane system.
choice_explanations: Incorrect. DNA replication occurs in the nucleus.
Correct. Ribosomes on rough ER make proteins that enter the endomembrane system.
Incorrect. Chlorophyll is found in chloroplasts.
Incorrect. That is not the role of the rough ER.

text: Why do many membrane proteins have nonpolar amino acids in the regions that span the bilayer?
choices: The membrane interior is strongly acidic
The membrane interior is made of carbohydrates
explain: The hydrophobic interior of the bilayer favors nonpolar amino acids in membrane-spanning regions.
choice_explanations: Correct. Nonpolar amino acids interact favorably with the nonpolar lipid tails.
Incorrect. Acidity is not the main explanation.
Incorrect. The key issue is hydrophobicity.
Incorrect. The membrane interior is mainly lipid tails.

text: A plant cell placed in distilled water becomes firm rather than bursting. Why?
choices: The cell wall resists excessive expansion
Plant cells do not take in water
The cell membrane becomes impermeable to water
explain: Water enters the plant cell, but the cell wall limits expansion and turgor pressure develops.
choice_explanations: Correct. Water enters, but the rigid cell wall limits swelling and creates turgor pressure.
Incorrect. Plant cells do take in water in hypotonic solutions.
Incorrect. The nucleus does not pump water out.
Incorrect. Water can still move across the membrane.

text: A vesicle buds from the ER and later fuses with the Golgi. What does this illustrate?
choices: Bulk transport within the endomembrane system
explain: Vesicles move cargo between organelles in the endomembrane system.
choice_explanations: Incorrect. DNA replication is unrelated here.
Correct. Vesicles move materials between organelles.
Incorrect. Osmosis is water diffusion across membranes.
Incorrect. This process uses vesicles, not channels.

text: Why can sodium ions not usually cross the phospholipid bilayer by simple diffusion?
choices: They are charged and do not pass easily through the hydrophobic interior
explain: Charged ions cannot pass easily through the hydrophobic core of the membrane and usually need transport proteins.
choice_explanations: Incorrect. Small size alone does not explain it.
Correct. Charged ions usually need transport proteins.
Incorrect. Sodium ions are not proteins.
Incorrect. They usually require channels or pumps, not necessarily vesicles.

text: A cell expends ATP to pump protons out of the cytoplasm. What does this indicate?
choices: Protons are moving down their gradient
Protons are moving against their gradient
explain: ATP use indicates active transport, so protons are being moved against their gradient.
choice_explanations: Incorrect. ATP use suggests movement against a gradient.
Correct. Active transport uses ATP to move substances against concentration or electrochemical gradients.
Incorrect. Osmosis refers to water movement.
Incorrect. Pumps, not vesicles, move protons this way.

text: A biologist observes that a cell has no nucleus and no membrane-bound organelles. Which conclusion is best supported?
explain: A lack of a nucleus and membrane-bound organelles is characteristic of prokaryotic cells.
choice_explanations: Correct. Prokaryotes lack a nucleus and membrane-bound organelles.
Incorrect. Plant cells are eukaryotic.
Incorrect. Prokaryotes still make proteins using ribosomes.
Incorrect. Lack of a nucleus does not mean the cell is dead.

text: Why are compartmentalized organelles beneficial in eukaryotic cells?
choices: They allow incompatible processes to occur in different microenvironments
They eliminate the need for enzymes
They prevent all transport within the cell
They make all cells the same size
explain: Compartmentalization separates processes into specialized spaces, increasing efficiency and control.
choice_explanations: Correct. Compartmentalization increases efficiency and control.
Incorrect. Organelles still rely on enzymes.
Incorrect. Cells still transport materials between organelles.
Incorrect. Organelles do not determine identical cell size.

text: A microscope image shows many flattened membrane sacs studded with ribosomes near the nucleus. Which organelle is most likely shown?
explain: Flattened sacs with ribosomes attached near the nucleus are characteristic of rough ER.
choice_explanations: Incorrect. Golgi sacs are not studded with ribosomes.
Correct. Rough ER appears as membrane sacs with ribosomes attached.
Incorrect. Mitochondria have inner folds called cristae.
Incorrect. Lysosomes are small vesicular organelles.

text: A drug makes the plasma membrane more rigid by reducing phospholipid movement. Which function would most likely be affected first?
explain: Reduced membrane fluidity most directly affects membrane behavior, including transport and vesicle fusion.
choice_explanations: Correct. Membrane fluidity affects transport proteins, vesicle fusion, and overall membrane behavior.
Incorrect. DNA base pairing is not a membrane function.
Incorrect. Ribosomes are not the first structure directly affected by membrane rigidity.
Incorrect. That is not the most direct membrane-related effect.

text: Why is a membrane called a fluid mosaic?
choices: It is made only of moving proteins
It contains a variety of molecules that can move laterally within the bilayer
It is built from repeating nucleotides
explain: Membranes are fluid because components move laterally and mosaic because they contain many different components.
choice_explanations: Incorrect. Membranes also contain phospholipids and other molecules.
Correct. “Fluid” refers to movement; “mosaic” refers to mixed components.
Incorrect. Membranes are dynamic, not permanently rigid.
Incorrect. Membranes are not built from nucleotides.

text: A student says, "Diffusion stops because molecules stop moving at equilibrium." Which response is best?
choices: Correct, because equilibrium means no motion
Incorrect, because molecules still move, but there is no net movement
Incorrect, because equilibrium happens only in living cells
explain: At equilibrium, particles still move randomly, but movement in each direction is balanced so there is no net change.
choice_explanations: Incorrect. Molecules continue moving randomly at equilibrium.
Correct. Equal movement in both directions produces dynamic equilibrium.
Incorrect. Diffusion does not require ATP.
Incorrect. Equilibrium can occur in nonliving systems too.

text: A protein made in the rough ER is destined for secretion. Which path is most likely?
choices: Rough ER → Golgi → vesicle → plasma membrane
explain: Secreted proteins usually move from rough ER to Golgi to transport vesicles and then to the plasma membrane.
choice_explanations: Correct. Secreted proteins usually move through the endomembrane system in this order.
Incorrect. That is not the secretory pathway.
Incorrect. Proteins for secretion are not first processed through mitochondria.
Incorrect. The nucleolus is not the correct step in the secretory pathway.

text: Which best explains why water moves into a cell placed in a hypotonic solution?
choices: The outside solution has lower solute concentration than the cell
The cell actively pumps water inward
Solutes are always leaving the cell
explain: In a hypotonic solution, the outside has lower solute concentration, so water enters the cell by osmosis.
choice_explanations: Correct. Water moves from higher free-water concentration to lower free-water concentration.
Incorrect. Water movement here is passive.
Incorrect. That is not the cause.
Incorrect. The driving factor is relative water potential/solute concentration.

text: A scientist blocks ATP production in a cell. Which transport process would be affected most directly?
explain: Active transport directly depends on ATP, unlike passive transport processes.
choice_explanations: Incorrect. Simple diffusion does not require ATP.
Incorrect. Facilitated diffusion is passive.
Correct. Active transport directly requires ATP.
Incorrect. Osmosis is passive.

text: Why do cells use vesicles for some forms of transport?
choices: Some materials are too large to pass directly through membrane proteins
Vesicles replace the need for membranes
Vesicles are only used in prokaryotes
explain: Bulk transport uses vesicles to move large particles or large quantities of material that cannot pass through transport proteins.
choice_explanations: Correct. Bulk transport moves large particles or large amounts of material.
Incorrect. Vesicles are membrane-bound.
Incorrect. Vesicular transport is associated mainly with eukaryotes.
Incorrect. Vesicles move many kinds of cargo, not only DNA.

text: A plant cell has abundant chloroplasts, a cell wall, and a large central vacuole. Which feature most directly helps maintain turgor pressure?
explain: The central vacuole stores water and is a major contributor to turgor pressure in plant cells.
choice_explanations: Correct. Water stored in the central vacuole contributes strongly to turgor pressure.
Incorrect. Ribosomes make proteins.
Incorrect. The nucleolus makes rRNA.
Incorrect. Smooth ER does not directly maintain turgor pressure.

text: A membrane channel is specific for potassium ions. Which statement best explains this specificity?
choices: The channel’s structure and chemical properties match potassium better than other substances
Potassium ions are the only ions in cells
Channels use ATP to force all ions through
explain: Transport protein specificity depends on size, shape, and charge compatibility with the transported molecule or ion.
choice_explanations: Correct. Selective transport proteins recognize certain size/charge characteristics.
Incorrect. Cells contain multiple ions.
Incorrect. Channels usually allow passive movement.
Incorrect. Potassium ions are positively charged.

text: A cell grows microvilli on its surface. What is the most likely benefit?
choices: Increased surface area for exchange or absorption
explain: Microvilli increase surface area, which improves absorption and exchange efficiency.
choice_explanations: Correct. Microvilli raise surface area-to-volume effectiveness without large volume increase.
Incorrect. Microvilli do not store DNA.
Incorrect. Their main benefit is increased area.
Incorrect. Microvilli are supported by the cytoskeleton.

text: A scientist compares a prokaryotic cell and a eukaryotic cell. Which structure is most likely present in both?
explain: Both prokaryotic and eukaryotic cells contain ribosomes for protein synthesis.
choice_explanations: Incorrect. Prokaryotes lack a nucleus.
Correct. Both prokaryotic and eukaryotic cells have ribosomes.
Incorrect. Prokaryotes do not have mitochondria.
Incorrect. Prokaryotes do not have a Golgi apparatus.

text: Why is cholesterol important in many animal cell membranes?
choices: It forms peptide bonds between proteins
It acts as the main ATP source
explain: Cholesterol helps stabilize membrane fluidity across temperature changes.
choice_explanations: Correct. Cholesterol can prevent membranes from becoming too rigid or too fluid.
Incorrect. Nucleic acids store hereditary information.
Incorrect. Cholesterol does not form peptide bonds.
Incorrect. Cholesterol is not the primary ATP source.

text: A cell takes in a large particle by surrounding it with membrane and bringing it inside. Which process is this?
explain: Endocytosis is the uptake of material into the cell through vesicle formation.
choice_explanations: Incorrect. Facilitated diffusion uses transport proteins for smaller substances.
Correct. Endocytosis uses vesicles to bring materials into the cell.
Incorrect. Exocytosis releases materials out of the cell.
Incorrect. Osmosis refers to water movement.

text: A molecule is small and nonpolar. Which statement is most likely true?
choices: It can usually cross the phospholipid bilayer more easily than a charged ion
It requires a vesicle to enter the cell
It always requires ATP to move
explain: Small nonpolar molecules often diffuse directly through the lipid bilayer.
choice_explanations: Correct. Small nonpolar molecules often diffuse directly through membranes.
Incorrect. Small nonpolar molecules usually do not need vesicles.
Incorrect. These molecules often cross readily.
Incorrect. Passive diffusion does not require ATP.

text: Why can an animal cell burst in a strongly hypotonic solution?
choices: Water enters the cell, and there is no rigid cell wall to prevent overexpansion
Water leaves the cell too quickly
explain: In a strongly hypotonic solution, water enters by osmosis and animal cells can lyse because they lack a rigid cell wall.
choice_explanations: Correct. Animal cells lack a rigid wall and may lyse.
Incorrect. In hypotonic solution, water enters.
Incorrect. That is not biologically accurate.
Incorrect. The immediate issue is osmotic water entry.

text: A biologist observes many mitochondria in a cell type. What is the best inference?
choices: The cell likely has high energy demands
The cell does not use membranes
explain: Cells with many mitochondria typically require large amounts of ATP.
choice_explanations: Correct. Mitochondria are associated with ATP production.
Incorrect. Mitochondria do not prevent protein synthesis.
Incorrect. Prokaryotes do not have mitochondria.
Incorrect. Mitochondria themselves are membrane-bound.

text: Students place equal-sized potato cores into three solutions for one hour. Solution 1 causes the core mass to increase, Solution 2 causes little to no change, and Solution 3 causes the core mass to decrease. Which interpretation is best?
choices: Solution 1 is hypotonic to the potato, Solution 2 is near isotonic, and Solution 3 is hypertonic
Solution 1 is hypertonic, Solution 2 is hypotonic, and Solution 3 is isotonic
The potato core in Solution 3 gained water
explain: A gain in mass indicates water entered the potato core, little change suggests isotonic conditions, and a loss in mass indicates water left the core.
choice_explanations: Correct. Water enters in hypotonic conditions, shows little net movement in isotonic conditions, and leaves in hypertonic conditions.
Incorrect. That does not match the mass changes.
Incorrect. The different mass changes show different tonicities.
Incorrect. A decrease in mass indicates water loss.
experiment: Equal-sized potato cores were placed in three different solutions for one hour, and mass changes were measured.

text: A class places dialysis tubing filled with starch solution into iodine solution. After some time, the bag turns blue-black, but the surrounding beaker does not. Which conclusion is best supported?
choices: Iodine moved into the bag, but starch did not move out
Starch moved out of the bag and iodine stayed outside
Neither iodine nor starch crossed the membrane
The membrane allowed only starch through
explain: The bag turning blue-black shows iodine entered and reacted with starch inside, while the beaker remaining unchanged suggests starch did not diffuse out.
choice_explanations: Correct. The color change inside the bag indicates iodine crossed the membrane and reacted with starch.
Incorrect. Then the beaker would likely change color.
Incorrect. The bag color change shows iodine entered.
Incorrect. The evidence indicates iodine crossed more easily than starch.
experiment: Dialysis tubing containing starch solution was submerged in iodine solution and color changes were observed inside and outside the tubing.

text: Researchers compare two cells of different sizes but similar shape. The smaller cell exchanges oxygen with its surroundings faster relative to its volume. Which explanation is best?
choices: The smaller cell has a higher surface area-to-volume ratio
The smaller cell has more DNA
The smaller cell lacks a membrane
Oxygen requires ATP to enter larger cells
explain: A higher surface area-to-volume ratio allows faster exchange relative to the cell’s size.
choice_explanations: Correct. This improves exchange relative to the cell’s size.
Incorrect. DNA amount is not the best explanation.
Incorrect. Cells still require membranes.
Incorrect. Oxygen usually diffuses passively.
experiment: Two similarly shaped cells of different sizes were compared for oxygen exchange rate relative to volume.

text: A scientist blocks ribosomes attached to the rough ER. Which cellular product is most directly reduced?
explain: Ribosomes on the rough ER synthesize proteins that enter the secretory pathway, including secreted proteins.
choice_explanations: Correct. Ribosomes on rough ER make proteins destined for secretion or membranes.
Incorrect. Ribosomes do not make nucleotides.
Incorrect. That is not the rough ER’s role.
Incorrect. Mitochondria produce ATP.
experiment: Ribosomes attached to rough ER were selectively inhibited and downstream cellular products were measured.

text: A membrane transport protein is mutated so that it no longer binds sodium ions well. Cells with the mutation accumulate abnormal sodium concentrations. Which conclusion is most reasonable?
choices: The mutation altered the protein’s structure enough to reduce transport specificity or efficiency
Sodium ions no longer exist outside the cell
The plasma membrane has turned into a cell wall
All diffusion in the cell has stopped
explain: Transport proteins depend on precise structure and chemistry to bind specific ions, so the mutation likely reduced sodium transport efficiency.
choice_explanations: Correct. Transport proteins depend on shape and chemistry to bind specific ions.
Incorrect. The mutation affects the protein, not sodium’s existence.
Incorrect. That is unrelated.
Incorrect. The mutation would affect a specific transport process.
experiment: A mutation was introduced into a sodium transport protein, and intracellular sodium concentration was measured.

text: Students compare red blood cells in three solutions under a microscope. In Beaker A the cells look normal, in Beaker B they are shriveled, and in Beaker C they are swollen. Which interpretation is best?
choices: A is isotonic, B is hypertonic, C is hypotonic
A is hypertonic, B is hypotonic, C is isotonic
A is hypotonic, B is isotonic, C is hypertonic
explain: Normal cells indicate isotonic conditions, shriveled cells indicate water loss in hypertonic solution, and swollen cells indicate water gain in hypotonic solution.
choice_explanations: Correct. Normal shape suggests isotonic, shriveling suggests water loss, and swelling suggests water gain.
Incorrect. That reverses the expected outcomes.
Incorrect. That does not fit the observed cell shapes.
Incorrect. The cells clearly responded differently.
experiment: Red blood cells were observed microscopically after being placed in three different solutions.

text: A plant cell is treated with a substance that disrupts the Golgi apparatus. Which process would be most directly affected?
choices: Modification and packaging of many proteins for transport
explain: The Golgi modifies, sorts, and packages proteins and lipids, so disrupting it directly affects those functions.
choice_explanations: Correct. The Golgi processes and sorts proteins and lipids.
Incorrect. DNA replication occurs in the nucleus.
Incorrect. Mitochondria are responsible for ATP production.
Incorrect. Osmosis is not the Golgi’s main function.
experiment: Cells were treated with a compound that disrupts the Golgi, and affected cellular processes were examined.

text: Researchers measure transport of a solute across a membrane. Transport continues even when the solute concentration inside becomes higher than outside, but stops when ATP production is blocked. Which conclusion is best supported?
choices: The solute is being actively transported
The solute is moving by simple diffusion
The solute is moving by osmosis
The solute is moving only through vesicles
explain: Movement against the gradient and ATP dependence are hallmark signs of active transport.
choice_explanations: Correct. Movement against the gradient and ATP dependence indicate active transport.
Incorrect. Simple diffusion cannot move against the gradient.
Incorrect. Osmosis applies to water.
Incorrect. The data specifically point to ATP-dependent transport, likely via pumps.
experiment: Transport of a solute was tracked across a membrane while concentration conditions changed and ATP production was experimentally blocked.

text: A student places two bags of dialysis tubing into water. Bag 1 contains concentrated sucrose. Bag 2 contains pure water. After an hour, Bag 1 gains mass while Bag 2 stays about the same. Which explanation is best?
choices: Water moved into Bag 1 by osmosis because Bag 1 had higher solute concentration
Sucrose moved out of Bag 1 faster than water entered
Water moved from low to high water concentration in Bag 2
explain: Water moved into the bag with higher solute concentration through the selectively permeable membrane.
choice_explanations: Correct. Water enters the more concentrated solution through a selectively permeable membrane.
Incorrect. Gaining mass suggests net water entry.
Incorrect. Dialysis tubing does not actively pump.
Incorrect. Bag 2 had little reason for net change.
experiment: Two dialysis bags with different internal solute concentrations were placed in water and their mass changes were measured.

text: A researcher studies two related proteins. Protein X is found in the cytoplasm and has many polar residues exposed on its surface. Protein Y is embedded in the membrane and has many nonpolar residues exposed on its surface. Which idea does this best support?
choices: Protein structure is related to the environment in which the protein functions
Membrane proteins are not made of amino acids
Cytoplasmic proteins cannot contain nonpolar amino acids
All proteins with similar functions have identical sequences
explain: Proteins tend to have surface properties that match the environment where they function, supporting stability and activity.
choice_explanations: Correct. Surface chemistry often matches the surrounding environment.
Incorrect. Membrane proteins are still proteins.
Incorrect. They can contain nonpolar amino acids internally.
Incorrect. Similar location or function does not require identical sequence.
experiment: Researchers compared amino acid surface properties of proteins located in cytoplasm versus membrane.

text: A cell line is exposed to a toxin that damages mitochondria. Which change is most likely observed first?
choices: Lower ATP availability for energy-demanding processes
Immediate loss of DNA from the nucleus
explain: Because mitochondria are central to ATP production, mitochondrial damage most directly lowers available ATP.
choice_explanations: Correct. Damaging mitochondria directly affects ATP production.
Incorrect. Mitochondrial damage does not immediately remove nuclear DNA.
Incorrect. Animal cells do not produce chlorophyll, and mitochondria are unrelated to it.
Incorrect. The most direct effect is reduced ATP supply.
experiment: A mitochondrial toxin was applied to cultured cells and the earliest cellular consequences were monitored.

text: Students graph the rate of diffusion of a dye through agar cubes of different sizes. Smaller cubes become fully colored much faster than larger cubes. Which conclusion is best supported?
choices: Smaller cubes have a higher surface area-to-volume ratio, improving diffusion relative to volume
Diffusion requires ATP in large cubes
Only small cubes can have membranes
explain: Smaller cubes have a higher surface area-to-volume ratio, so diffusion reaches the interior more efficiently.
choice_explanations: Correct. This is why smaller cells exchange materials more efficiently.
Incorrect. Larger cubes contain more total material, not less.
Incorrect. Diffusion is passive.
Incorrect. Cube size does not determine the presence of membranes in this model.
experiment: A dye diffusion model using agar cubes of different sizes was used to compare diffusion efficiency.

text: A scientist mutates a channel protein so that the pore becomes narrower. Water still crosses the membrane, but a certain ion no longer passes through efficiently. Which explanation is best?
choices: The altered pore likely reduced the channel’s fit for that ion
Water and ions always use the exact same transport mechanism
The ion must have become nonpolar
The membrane is no longer selectively permeable
explain: The narrower pore likely changed the channel’s size or chemical compatibility for that ion, reducing its passage.
choice_explanations: Correct. Channel specificity depends on size and chemical compatibility.
Incorrect. Water and ions can move differently.
Incorrect. The mutation altered the protein, not the ion.
Incorrect. The membrane still shows selectivity.
experiment: A channel protein pore was narrowed by mutation, and transport of water and a specific ion was compared.

text: A cell is secreting large amounts of digestive enzymes. Which organelles would likely be especially abundant?
explain: Secretory cells need rough ER for protein synthesis and Golgi for modification and packaging.
choice_explanations: Correct. Secreted proteins are synthesized on rough ER and processed through the Golgi.
Incorrect. Those are plant structures and not key to enzyme secretion in this context.
Incorrect. They are not the main organelles for secretory protein processing.
Incorrect. Those are not the primary secretory pathway components.
experiment: A highly secretory cell type was examined for organelle abundance relative to its function.

text: A researcher places cells in a solution and observes no net water movement. Which conclusion is best supported?
choices: The solution is isotonic relative to the cells
The cells have stopped all molecular motion
explain: No net water movement indicates isotonic conditions, even though water molecules continue moving both directions.
choice_explanations: Correct. No net osmosis suggests equal water potential on both sides.
Incorrect. Hypertonic conditions would cause net water loss.
Incorrect. Hypotonic conditions would cause net water gain.
Incorrect. Molecules still move at equilibrium.
experiment: Cells were placed in an unknown solution and monitored for net water movement.

text: A cell cannot form lysosomes properly. Which problem is most likely to result?
choices: Reduced ability to digest and recycle cellular materials
explain: Lysosomes digest and recycle materials, so their absence most directly reduces cellular breakdown and recycling.
choice_explanations: Correct. Lysosomes break down macromolecules and worn-out components.
Incorrect. DNA replication is not the main lysosome function.
Incorrect. Lysosomes do not create the main plasma membrane.
Incorrect. Ribosomes are assembled with help from the nucleolus.
experiment: Cells lacking proper lysosome formation were analyzed for functional defects.

text: Scientists compare two solutions separated by a selectively permeable membrane that allows water through but not solute. Water moves toward the side with more solute. Which principle does this demonstrate?
choices: Osmosis is driven by differences in solute concentration across a membrane
Solutes always cross faster than water
explain: Water moves across a selectively permeable membrane toward the side with higher solute concentration due to osmosis.
choice_explanations: Correct. Water tends to move toward the side with lower free-water concentration.
Incorrect. Water movement here is passive.
Incorrect. Osmosis does not require vesicles.
Incorrect. The membrane in this setup does not allow solute through.
experiment: Two solutions with different solute concentrations were separated by a membrane permeable to water but not solute.

text: A mutant eukaryotic cell cannot package proteins into transport vesicles after Golgi modification. Which process is most directly impaired?
choices: Delivery of many proteins to the membrane or outside the cell
Replication of DNA in the nucleus
Formation of peptide bonds on ribosomes
Diffusion of oxygen through the membrane
explain: If Golgi-processed proteins cannot be packaged into vesicles, they cannot be delivered efficiently to the membrane or secreted.
choice_explanations: Correct. Vesicles are needed to ship Golgi-processed products to their destinations.
Incorrect. DNA replication is not the direct role of transport vesicles.
Incorrect. Translation occurs earlier in the pathway.
Incorrect. Oxygen diffuses directly and does not need vesicles.
experiment: A mutant cell line defective in Golgi vesicle packaging was examined for downstream transport problems.
